summary: The backend follows a conventional layered architecture (Presentation, Service, Repository, Domain) built with Spring Boot and related Spring stack technologies, exposing REST APIs for deed and document management. Functionally it delivers a rich set of capabilities around CRUD operations, history tracking, archiving, key management, reporting, and security. Quality-wise the codebase shows moderate separation, coupling and cohesion with notable technical debt such as an excessive number of entity classes, missing abstractions, and oversized controllers, resulting in an overall grade of C.

integrations: Relational Database (via Spring Data JPA / Hibernate)

functional.bounded_context: Deed and Document Management with Archiving and Key Management
capabilities: Create, read, update, delete (CRUD) deed entries
Log and retrieve deed entry history
Manage action streams and action types
Perform archiving operations and sign submission/reencryption tokens
Manage cryptographic keys and re‑encryption processes
Security enforcement via custom method expressions

debt_indicators: Large number of entity classes (199) suggesting over‑modeling
Many service and DAO implementations used directly (Impl suffix) without interfaces
Mix of persistence and business logic in some service classes
recommendations: Introduce explicit interfaces for services and repositories to decouple layers
Refactor oversized controllers into smaller, focused endpoint handlers
Consolidate related entity classes or apply bounded‑context partitioning to improve domain cohesion
Apply Facade or Application Service pattern for external integrations (KeyManager, Archiving) to isolate third‑party APIs
Consider adopting domain‑driven design aggregates to reduce anemic domain model and improve encapsulation

overall_assessment: The backend provides a comprehensive functional set and uses a familiar Spring‑based stack, but architectural concerns such as excessive entity proliferation, lack of proper abstractions, and monolithic controllers hinder maintainability and scalability, reflected in a moderate quality rating.
top_recommendations: Introduce explicit interfaces for services and repositories to decouple layers
Refactor oversized controllers into smaller, focused endpoint handlers
Consolidate related entity classes or apply bounded‑context partitioning to improve domain cohesion
